title: Van Eyck, Ghent Altarpiece
description: This quiz covers two videos: Jan van Eyck, _The Ghent Altarpiece_ (closed), 1432 and Jan van Eyck, _The Ghent Altarpiece_ (open), 1432.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The *Ghent Altarpiece* is a [[☃ dropdown 1]], which is made of many panels that can open and close.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fea97b1a4481cc3352a2086026585aa3.png)
hints:
**The *Ghent Altarpiece* is a polyptych, which is made of many panels that can open and close.**

**[Watch this to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/flanders/v/ghent-altarpiece-closed)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the closed view, prophets and sylbils are present in the upper sections of the altarpiece. These figures prophecy of the end of the world.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4db3589e5b91fec3d7ccd517f0803dcb.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**In the closed view, prophets and sylbils are present in the upper sections of the altarpiece. These figures prophecy the coming of a messiah (a savior).**

**[Watch this to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/flanders/v/ghent-altarpiece-closed)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of the *Ghent Altarpiece* (closed)?**

 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fea97b1a4481cc3352a2086026585aa3.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Artists of the Northern Renaissance were especially interested in representing the details of the natural world.**

**[Watch this to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/flanders/v/ghent-altarpiece-closed)**

choices.content: The Annunciation, foretold by the prophets and sibyls, takes place in the central panels. The Angel Gabriel visits Mary to tell her she will have a baby.
The artists are not using linear perspective or representing the human body naturalistically.
There is tension between the writing, which is a medieval convention, and the Renaissance realism of the scene.
Artists of the Northern Renaissance were not interested in the details of the natural world, but more interested in a sense of generalized realism.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the center of the center panel, [[☃ dropdown 1]] is dressed like a king with the papal crown on his head and another crown at his feet.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1974417cd86701cc70dcedf823278592.png)
hints:
**In the center of the center panel, God the Father is dressed like a king with the papal crown on his head and another crown at his feet. Some art historians have noted that this figure could also represent Christ.**

**[Watch this to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/flanders/v/ghent-altar-open)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**God's saving grace and power are themes that run through the altarpiece. This is a [[☃ dropdown 1]] idea as opposed to the wrathful God of [[☃ dropdown 2]] art.**





![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5087f6502571d7bb11ca18c2f6702e2a.png)
hints:
**God's saving grace and power are a theme that run throughout the altarpiece, which is a newer Renaissance idea as opposed to the wrathful God of medieval art.**

**[Watch this to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/flanders/v/ghent-altar-open)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of the *Ghent Altarpiece* (open)?**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5087f6502571d7bb11ca18c2f6702e2a.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**In the scene below, four groups of people come to the altar with the lamb (not a goat) bleeding into the chalice, which is a symbol of Christ and his sacrifice.**

**[Watch this to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/flanders/v/ghent-altar-open)**

choices.content: In representing a forgiving God, the van Eyck brothers are showing the influence of Humanism.
The fabric of God's throne shows the image of the pelican, who is feeding its children on its own flesh and blood. This is an image of God's love and forgiveness.
The angels playing instruments  and others who are singing recall the sounds of heaven.
In the scene below, four groups of people come to the altar with the goat bleeding into the chalice, which is a symbol of Mary and her sacrifice.
